4 day Amboseli Tsavo East And Tsavo West:
Safari Overview 
This 4-day safari offers a compact yet diverse journey through southern Kenya’s most scenic and 
wildlife-rich destinations. Covering the iconic plains of Amboseli National Park, the dramatic 
volcanic landscapes of Tsavo West National Park, and the exclusive setting of Taita Hills Wildlife 
Sanctuary, the itinerary delivers a well-rounded safari experience in a short time. 
Guests will enjoy close encounters with large elephant herds set against the breathtaking backdrop of 
Mount Kilimanjaro, explore natural attractions such as Mzima Springs, and experience varied 
ecosystems ranging from open savannah to rugged hills and waterhole environments. 
The safari combines authentic game viewing, scenic beauty, and comfortable lodge stays, 
including the unique experience of overnighting at an elevated lodge overlooking a floodlit 
waterhole perfect for continuous wildlife viewing even after sunset. 
Ideal for travelers with limited time, this itinerary captures the essence of Kenya’s wilderness while 
maintaining a relaxed and enjoyable pace. 
4-Day Amboseli & Tsavo Safari 
Route: Nairobi – Amboseli – Tsavo West – Taita Hills – Nairobi 
Accommodation: 
 1 Night – Amboseli Sopa Lodge  
 1 Night – Kilaguni Serena Safari Lodge  
 1 Night – Salt Lick Safari Lodge  
 
Day 1: Nairobi – Amboseli National Park 
 Early morning pick-up from hotel/airport  
 Drive via Emali to Amboseli National Park  
 Enjoy scenic views en route, with sightings of Maasai homesteads  
Arrival: 
 Arrive in time for lunch at Amboseli Sopa Lodge  
 Relax and enjoy lodge facilities  
Afternoon Activity: 
 Game drive in Amboseli, famous for:  
o Large elephant herds  
o Stunning backdrop of Mount Kilimanjaro  
o Lions, cheetahs, buffalo, and plains game  
Evening: 
 Dinner and overnight at the lodge  
 
 
Day 2: Amboseli – Tsavo West 
 Early morning game drive (best chance for clear Kilimanjaro views)  
 Return for breakfast  
 Depart for Tsavo West National Park  
En Route: 
 Game drive as you enter the park  
 Visit the famous Mzima Springs  
o Crystal-clear water with hippos and crocodiles  
Arrival: 
 Lunch at Kilaguni Serena Safari Lodge overlooking a waterhole  
Afternoon: 
 Game drive exploring lava flows, volcanic hills, and wildlife  

Evening: 
 Dinner and overnight at the lodge  
 
 
Day 3: Tsavo West – Taita Hills (Salt Lick) 
 Early breakfast  
 Short morning game drive in Tsavo West  
 Depart for Taita Hills Wildlife Sanctuary  
Arrival: 
 Arrive at Salt Lick in time for lunch  
Afternoon Activity: 
 Game drive within the private sanctuary  
 Excellent chances to see:  
o Elephants  
o Buffalo  
o Lions and other predators  

Unique Experience: 
 Elevated lodge overlooking a floodlit waterhole  
 Night wildlife viewing directly from the lodge  
Evening: 
 Dinner and overnight  
 
 
Day 4: Taita Hills – Nairobi 
 Early morning game drive or relaxed breakfast  
 Depart for Nairobi  
En Route: 
 Lunch stop (optional)  
Arrival: 
 Drop-off at hotel or airport in the afternoon
4 day Nairobi-Amboseli Itinerary (1).pdf:
4-Day Nairobi – Amboseli Safari (2026) 
(1 night PrideInn Westlands Nairobi + 2 nights Kibo Safari Camp Amboseli) 
 
Day 1 – Arrival in Nairobi 
Arrive at Jomo Kenyatta International Airport where our representative meets you and 
transfers you to PrideInn Westlands for check-in and safari briefing. Relax at the hotel or 
explore the city at your leisure. 
What to See: 
 Nairobi’s lively Westlands area with cafés, restaurants and shopping. 
Overnight at PrideInn Westlands Nairobi. 
 
 
Day 2 – Nairobi to Amboseli National Park & Afternoon Game Drive 
After breakfast depart by road in a shared 4×4 Land Cruiser for Amboseli National Park at 
the foot of Mount Kilimanjaro. Arrive and check in at Kibo Safari Camp, then head out for 
an afternoon game drive in the park. 

What to See: 
 Changing scenery as you drive south from Nairobi: highlands, small towns, and 
Maasai homesteads. 
 Iconic backdrop of Mount Kilimanjaro, Africa’s highest peak, towering over the 
park. 
 Large herds of African elephants — Amboseli is famous for its tuskers. 
 Plains game including zebras, wildebeest, buffalo, giraffes, hippos in swamps, and 
predators such as lions and hyenas. 
 Birdlife around the marshes: African jacana, egrets, crowned cranes. 
 Stunning sunset photos with wildlife in silhouette against Kilimanjaro. 
Dinner and overnight at Kibo Safari Camp Amboseli. 
 
 

 
Day 3 – Full-Day Game Drive in Amboseli 
Enjoy a full-day game drive exploring Amboseli’s diverse habitats: open plains, swamps and 
acacia woodland. A picnic lunch is served at a scenic spot inside the park before continuing 
your exploration. 
What to See: 
 Early morning view of Kilimanjaro’s snow-capped peak before clouds gather. 
 Large elephant herds crossing the plains, often with calves. 
 Predator activity at dawn — lions, cheetahs, and sometimes servals. 
 Hippos and crocodiles near the marshes. 
 Over 400 bird species, from pelicans and kingfishers to raptors. 
 Photogenic scenes of wildlife with Kilimanjaro as a backdrop. 
Dinner and overnight at Kibo Safari Camp Amboseli. 
 
 

Day 4 – Amboseli to Nairobi (No Game Drive) 
After breakfast check out and drive back to Nairobi, enjoying views of the countryside and 
making brief stops at roadside markets or viewpoints. On arrival you will be dropped at the 
airport for your flight back home. 
What to See: 
 Morning light over Kilimanjaro as you leave Amboseli. 
 Rural Kenyan landscapes with Maasai grazing livestock. 
 Souvenir markets with colorful crafts along the highway. 
 
 
Inclusions 
 1-night accommodation at PrideInn Westlands Nairobi (bed & breakfast). 
 2 nights’ accommodation at Kibo Safari Camp Amboseli (full board). 
 All airport and hotel transfers in Nairobi. 
 Transport in a shared 4×4 safari Land Cruiser with pop-up roof. 
 1 half-day + 1 full-day game drive in Amboseli. 
 Amboseli park entry fees. 
 Bottled water in the safari vehicle. 
Exclusions 
 Drinks at lodges, tips, personal expenses. 
 Optional activities such as hot air balloon safari (not offered in Amboseli) or extra 
Nairobi tours. 
 

Amboseli Seasons by Month 
Months Season & What to Expect 
January–March Warm, mostly dry; clear Kilimanjaro views; elephants plentiful, 
predator sightings good. 
April–June Long rains; greener scenery, more bird species, fewer tourists. Roads 
may be muddy. 
July–October Cool, dry; best time for large herds at swamps, skies clearer for 
Kilimanjaro photos. 
November–
December 
Short rains; lush grass, dramatic skies, birding excellent, young 
animals around.
4 day Nairobi-Mara Itinerary (1).pdf:
4-Day Nairobi – Masai Mara Group Safari (2026) 
(1-night Pride Inn Westland’s Nairobi + 2 nights Pride Inn Mara Camp) 
 
Day 1 – Arrival in Nairobi 
Arrive at Jomo Kenyatta International Airport and be met by our representative for a private 
transfer to Pride Inn Westland’s. After check-in and a short safari briefing, you can relax at 
the hotel or explore Nairobi. 
What to See: 
 Nairobi’s leafy Westland’s district with its vibrant restaurants and shops. 
 Lively Nairobi nightlife and dining scene if you wish to go out. 
Overnight at Pride Inn Westland’s, Nairobi. 
 

 
 
Day 2 – Nairobi to Masai Mara & Afternoon Game Drive 
Travel by road through the Great Rift Valley escarpment to reach the world-famous Masai 
Mara National Reserve. Check into PrideInn Mara Camp and head out for an afternoon game 
drive. 
What to See: 
 Panoramic views of the Great Rift Valley with its dramatic cliffs and far-off lakes. 
 Vast golden savannah dotted with flat-topped acacia trees. 
 Large herds of plains game such as zebra, wildebeest, giraffe, topi and impala. 
 Possible sightings of big cats — lions lounging on termite mounds, cheetahs scanning 
the plains, and leopards resting in fig trees. 
 Abundant birdlife, including lilac-breasted rollers, secretary birds and crowned 
cranes. 
Dinner and overnight at Pride Inn Mara Camp. 

 
 
 
Day 3 – Full-Day Game Drive & Maasai Village Visit 
Spend a full day exploring different parts of the Masai Mara on an extended game drive, with 
a picnic lunch inside the reserve. In the afternoon visit a Maasai village for a cultural 
experience before returning to the camp. 
What to See: 

 Early morning wildlife activity when predators are most active — lions, hyenas, and 
sometimes a leopard dragging a kill. 
 Hippos wallowing in pools, crocodiles sunning themselves along the Mara River, and 
elephants with their calves. 
 The dramatic scenery of rolling hills, winding rivers and endless plains. 
 At the Maasai Village: warriors performing traditional dances, manyattas 
(homesteads) built from mud and sticks, beadwork and handicrafts, and a glimpse into 
the Maasai’s pastoral lifestyle. 
Dinner and overnight at PrideInn Mara Camp. 
 
 

Day 4 – Masai Mara to Nairobi (No Game Drive) 
After breakfast, check out and drive back to Nairobi. En route enjoy the beautiful scenery and 
make brief stops at viewpoints or local markets. On arrival in Nairobi you’ll be transferred to 
the airport for your flight back home. 
What to See: 
 Changing landscapes as you leave the Mara: savannah giving way to highlands and 
farmland. 
 Scenic escarpments of the Great Rift Valley. 
 Roadside markets with colorful handicrafts and curios. 
 
 
               
 

Masai Mara Seasons by Month 
Months        Season & What to Expect 
January–March 
Warm, mostly dry season; grass shorter; great visibility and predator 
sightings. Many newborn animals. 
 
April–June 
Long rains; greener landscapes and fewer tourists. Birdlife excellent. 
Roads can be muddy but photography stunning. 
 
July–October 
Peak season for the Great Wildebeest Migration. Herds cross the Mara 
River (spectacular but busy with visitors). Dry and sunny. 
 
November–
December 
Short rains; fresh green plains, dramatic skies and superb birding. Baby 
animals appear in December. 
 
 
 
Inclusions 
 1-night accommodation at Pride Inn Westland’s Nairobi (bed & breakfast). 
 2 nights’ accommodation at Pride Inn Mara Camp (full board). 
 Shared 4×4 Land Cruiser with pop-up roof & driver-guide. 
 Airport/hotel transfers in Nairobi. 
 1 half-day + 1 full-day game drive in the Mara. 
 Masai Mara park entry fees. 
 Maasai Village cultural visit. 
 Bottled water in the vehicle. 
Exclusions 
 Drinks at lodges, tips, personal expenses. 
 Optional activities such as hot air balloon safari or extra Nairobi tours.
5 day Mara-Nakuru Itinerary (1).pdf:
5-Day Masai Mara – Lake Nakuru Safari (2026) 
(1-night Pride Inn Westland’s Nairobi + 2 nights Mara Sopa Lodge + 1-night Lake Nakuru 
Sopa Lodge) 
 
Day 1 – Arrival in Nairobi 
Arrive at Jomo Kenyatta International Airport where you’ll be met by our representative and 
transferred to Pride Inn Westlands. After check-in, receive a safari briefing and relax at the 
hotel or explore Nairobi at leisure. 
What to See / Optional Activities: 
 Nairobi’s vibrant Westlands district. 
Dinner and overnight at Pride Inn Westlands. 
 
 
Day 2 – Nairobi to Masai Mara & Afternoon Game Drive 
Depart Nairobi in a 4×4 safari Land Cruiser through the Great Rift Valley escarpment to the 
world-famous Masai Mara National Reserve. Arrive and check in at Mara Sopa Lodge 
then head out for an afternoon game drive. 

What to See / Activities: 
 Scenic drive with views of the Rift Valley floor and distant lakes. 
 Afternoon game drive across rolling savannahs dotted with acacia trees. 
 Abundant plains game: zebras, wildebeest, giraffes, topis and impalas. 
 Big cats – lions basking on mounds, cheetahs scanning the plains, elusive leopards in 
fig trees. 
 Birdlife such as lilac-breasted rollers and secretary birds. 
 Iconic Mara sunsets. 
Dinner and overnight at Mara Sopa Lodge. 
 
 

Day 3 – Full-Day Game Drive in Masai Mara 
Enjoy a full-day game drive exploring the different parts of Masai Mara. A picnic lunch is 
served at a scenic spot inside the reserve. 
What to See / Activities: 
 Early morning predator activity — lions, hyenas, and sometimes leopards with their 
kills. 
 Herds of elephants with calves, buffalo and hippos near the Mara River. 
 Crocodiles sunning along riverbanks. 
 Rolling hills and endless plains teeming with wildlife. 
 Seasonal wildebeest migration (July–October). 
 Abundant birdlife: crowned cranes, ostriches, raptors. 
 Optional balloon safari (extra cost) at dawn for aerial views of the reserve. 
Dinner and overnight at Mara Sopa Lodge. 
 
 
Day 4 – Masai Mara to Lake Nakuru National Park 
After breakfast depart for Lake Nakuru National Park, arriving at Lake Nakuru Sopa 
Lodge. Later head out for an afternoon game drive in the park. 
What to See / Activities: 

 Changing scenery as you leave the Mara and ascend the Rift Valley escarpment. 
 Lake Nakuru’s shimmering soda lake fringed by acacia woodland. 
 Famous for rhino conservation — good chance of seeing black and white rhinos. 
 Lions and leopards in the trees, buffalo herds grazing by the lake. 
 Large populations of pelicans and cormorants, plus seasonal flamingos. 
 Scenic viewpoints such as Baboon Cliff for panoramic photos. 
Dinner and overnight at Lake Nakuru Sopa Lodge. 
 
 

Day 5 – Lake Naivasha Crescent Island Walking Safari & Return to Nairobi 
Depart Lake Nakuru and drive to Lake Naivasha. Enjoy a boat ride on the lake spotting 
hippos and birdlife, followed by a guided walking safari on Crescent Island Sanctuary 
among freely roaming herbivores. Later drive back to Nairobi for your airport or hotel drop-
off. 
What to See / Activities: 
 Boat ride on Lake Naivasha with close-up views of hippos and fish eagles swooping 
for prey. 
 Crescent Island guided walk where giraffes, zebras and antelopes roam without 
predators. 
 Lush lakeside vegetation and scenic Rift Valley escarpments. 
 Souvenir markets en route back to Nairobi. 
 

 
Inclusions 
 1-night accommodation at PrideInn Westlands Nairobi (bed & breakfast). 
 2 nights’ accommodation at Mara Sopa Lodge (full board). 
 1-night accommodation at Lake Nakuru Sopa Lodge (full board). 
 Airport/hotel transfers in Nairobi. 
 Transport in a shared 4×4 safari Land Cruiser with pop-up roof. 
 2 game drives in Masai Mara (1 half-day + 1 full-day). 
 1 game drive in Lake Nakuru National Park. 
 Park fees for Masai Mara Game Reserve & Lake Nakuru National Park. 
 Boat ride on Lake Naivasha. 
 Walking safari on Crescent Island Sanctuary. 
 Bottled water in the safari vehic
7 day Mara-Nakuru-Amboseli Itinerary (1):
7-Day Masai Mara – Lake Nakuru – Amboseli Safari 
(2026) 
(1 night PrideInn Westlands Nairobi + 2 nights Mara Sopa Lodge + 1-night Lake Nakuru 
Sopa Lodge + 2 nights Amboseli Sopa Lodge) 
 
Day 1 – Arrival in Nairobi 
Meet & greet at Jomo Kenyatta International Airport and transfer to PrideInn Westlands. 
After check-in, get a safari briefing and relax or explore Nairobi at leisure. 
What to See / Optional Activities: 
 Nairobi’s lively Westlands district. 
Dinner and overnight at PrideInn Westlands. 
 
 
Day 2 – Nairobi to Masai Mara & Afternoon Game Drive 
Depart by road in a 4×4 safari Land Cruiser through the Great Rift Valley escarpment to 
Masai Mara National Reserve. Check in at Mara Sopa Lodge and later head out for an 
afternoon game drive. 

What to See / Activities: 
 Scenic escarpment viewpoints with sweeping Rift Valley vistas. 
 Golden savannah dotted with flat-topped acacias. 
 Plains game: zebras, wildebeest, topis, giraffes. 
 Big cats – lions, cheetahs, leopards. 
 Birdlife including lilac-breasted rollers and vultures. 
Dinner and overnight at Mara Sopa Lodge. 
 
 
 
 

Day 3 – Full-Day Game Drive in Masai Mara & Maasai Village Visit 
Full-day game drive exploring different sectors of the Masai Mara. Picnic lunch inside the 
reserve. In the afternoon visit a Maasai cultural village to experience the traditional 
lifestyle. 
What to See / Activities: 
 Early morning predator activity — lions roaring, hyenas on the move, chance of 
seeing a leopard. 
 Herds of elephants, buffalo and hippos near the Mara River. 
 Crocodiles basking on riverbanks. 
 Seasonal wildebeest migration (July–October). 
 Birdlife: crowned cranes, ostriches, raptors. 
 At the Maasai village: warrior dances, manyattas (traditional huts), beadwork and 
handicrafts. 
Dinner and overnight at Mara Sopa Lodge. 
 
 
Day 4 – Masai Mara to Lake Nakuru National Park 
After breakfast depart for Lake Nakuru National Park, arriving at Lake Nakuru Sopa 
Lodge. In the afternoon enjoy a game drive in the park. 
What to See / Activities: 
 Changing landscapes as you ascend the Rift Valley escarpment. 
 Lake Nakuru’s alkaline waters fringed by acacia woodland. 
 Rhino sanctuary — high chance of spotting both black and white rhinos. 

 Lions resting in yellow-barked acacias, leopards occasionally spotted. 
 Huge flocks of pelicans and cormorants; flamingos seasonally present. 
 Scenic viewpoints such as Baboon Cliff. 
Dinner and overnight at Lake Nakuru Sopa Lodge. 
 
 
Day 5 – Lake Nakuru to Amboseli National Park & Afternoon Game Drive 
After breakfast drive south to Amboseli National Park at the foot of Mount Kilimanjaro. 
Check in at Amboseli Sopa Lodge and head out for an afternoon game drive. 
What to See / Activities: 
 Transition from highlands to the open plains of Amboseli. 
 Iconic view of Mount Kilimanjaro towering above the park. 
 Large herds of elephants — Amboseli is famed for its tuskers. 
 Plains game, buffalo, lions and hyenas. 

 Birdlife around the swamps. 
 Sunset photography with wildlife silhouetted against Kilimanjaro. 
Dinner and overnight at Amboseli Sopa Lodge. 
 
 
Day 6 – Full-Day Game Drive in Amboseli 
Spend a full day exploring Amboseli’s open plains, marshes and acacia woodlands with a 
picnic lunch inside the park. 
What to See / Activities: 
 Clear morning views of Kilimanjaro’s snow-capped peak. 
 Elephant herds crossing the plains. 
 Predators active at dawn – lions, cheetahs, jackals, hyenas. 
 Hippos and crocodiles near the marshes. 
 Over 400 bird species, including pelicans, crowned cranes and raptors. 
 Classic photo scenes of wildlife with Kilimanjaro backdrop. 
Dinner and overnight at Amboseli Sopa Lodge. 

 
 
Day 7 – Amboseli to Nairobi (Departure) 
After breakfast check out and drive back to Nairobi, enjoying scenic views and roadside 
markets. Drop off at the airport for your flight back home. 
What to See / Activities: 
 Morning light over Kilimanjaro. 
 Rural landscapes
